info.name: Pawffy Vendor API
info.description:
Vendor / Partner app backend — {{baseUrl}}

## Recommended test flow
1. **Auth** → Register Vendor or Login Vendor (saves `token`)
2. **Onboarding** → business → services → availability → documents → review → submit
3. **Admin Verification** → Login Admin (saves `adminToken`) → List Pending → Approve
4. **Main app** → Home, Requests, Calendar, Messages, Profile
5. **Account & Security** → request phone/email change → complete verification → refreshed `token`

## Auth
- Vendor routes: `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}` (role: partner)
- Admin routes: `Authorization: Bearer {{adminToken}}` (role: admin)
- Vendor login: `POST /api/auth/vendor/login`
- Admin login: `POST /api/auth/login` (NOT vendor login)

## Onboarding vs live routes
| Action | Before approval | After approval |
|---|---|---|
| Availability | `PUT /api/vendor/onboarding/availability` | `PUT /api/vendor/availability` |
| Services CRUD | `/api/vendor/onboarding/services` | `/api/vendor/services` |

Onboarding routes return 400 once `verificationStatus` is `verified`.

## Account & Security notes
- In non-production, phone OTP and email verification token may be returned in API responses for local testing.
- In production, those values are **not** returned by the API.
- Production phone verification expects the OTP to be delivered by your SMS channel.
- Production email verification expects Twilio SendGrid (or SMTP) delivery of a 6-digit OTP to the new email address.
- After successful phone/email verification, the API returns a fresh JWT and older sessions become invalid because `tokenVersion` is incremented.

## Password policy
Min 8 chars · uppercase + lowercase + digit

## Collection variables
token · adminToken · userId · businessId · serviceId · documentId · requestId · blockedDateId · conversationId · receiverId · vendorEmail · vendorPassword · adminEmail · adminPassword · newVendorEmail · newVendorPhone · emailOtp · phoneOtp

request.description: Creates partner user + draft business profile. Requires `acceptTerms: true`.
Partner accounts only. Returns 403 for customer/admin accounts.

exec: if (!r.data?.token) console.warn('No token returned — check OTP / environment');

request.description: Completes vendor login using the SMS OTP. In production, paste the OTP into `phoneOtp` first.

item.name: 2. Onboarding (before approval)
item.description:
Use only while `verificationStatus` is `incomplete` or `rejected`.
Blocked once status is `pending` or `verified`.

body.raw:
{
  "businessName": "Max Pet Care",
  "contactName": "Amit Patel",
  "phone": "+919652949690",
  "location": "Ghaziabad, UP",
  "description": "Trusted pet grooming and care services"
}

{
  "serviceType": "groomer",
  "name": "Full Grooming",
  "description": "Complete grooming package",
  "inclusions": ["Bath", "Haircut", "Nail Trim", "Blow dry", "Ear Cleaning"],
  "durationMinutes": 60,
  "priceType": "fixed",
  "price": 40,
  "serviceLocation": "at_my_place"
}

request.description: serviceType: vet | groomer | walker | trainer | sitter | boarding | transport | poop_scooper
⚠️ Onboarding only. After approval use Calendar → Manage Availability.

formdata.description: JPEG, PNG, WebP or PDF · max 5MB

request.description: Sets verificationStatus to `pending`.
Available while verification is pending.

item.name: 3. Admin Verification
item.description:
1. Set `adminEmail` + `adminPassword` in collection variables
2. Run Login Admin → saves `adminToken`
3. List Pending → saves `businessId`
4. Approve Application

exec: if (r.data?.user?.role !== 'admin') console.warn('User is not admin — approval will fail');

request.description: Regular login — NOT `/api/auth/vendor/login`.

exec: if (!r.data?.token) console.warn('No adminToken returned — check OTP / environment');

request.description: Completes admin login using the SMS OTP. In production, paste the OTP into `phoneOtp` first.

item.name: 5. Requests
item.description:
Locked until `verificationStatus` is `verified`.
status: pending | upcoming | completed | canceled

request.description: ✅ Use after vendor is approved.
✅ Use after vendor is approved. Do NOT use `/onboarding/availability`.

item.name: 8. Profile & Services
item.description: After approval, manage services via `/api/vendor/services` (not onboarding).

request.description: period: week | month | year

body.raw:
{
  "contactName": "Aman Patel",
  "businessName": "Max Pet Care",
  "phone": "+919652949690",
  "location": "Delhi, India",
  "city": "Delhi",
  "state": "India",
  "profileTitle": "Pet Groomer & Vet",
  "description": "Professional pet care services"
}

request.description: Multipart upload: form-data field name must be `avatar`.
✅ After approval. Onboarding route blocked once verified.

item.name: 8.5 Account & Security
item.description:
Phase 2 vendor account-security flows.

Non-production: the API may return `phoneOtp` / `emailOtp` and the collection scripts will save them automatically.

Production: the API will not return those secrets. Use the OTP received over SMS and the email OTP delivered to the new email address, then paste the real value into the corresponding collection variable before running the verify request.

request.description: Starts a secure phone update. In production, expect the OTP to be delivered outside the API response.
Completes phone verification using the real OTP. In production, paste the SMS OTP into `phoneOtp` first.
Starts a secure email change. In non-production the API may return `otp`. In production the OTP is delivered by Twilio SendGrid (or SMTP fallback) and is not returned.
Completes the email swap using the 6-digit OTP. In production, paste the OTP from the email into `emailOtp` first.

item.name: 8.6 Adoptions
item.description: Vendor adoption workflow for pet-based applications. Use the wallet payment request only when wallet payments are enabled in the backend; otherwise use the Stripe intent + verify sequence.

request.description: Upload one document at a time using field name `document`. Supported types mirror other vendor document uploads.
Use only when `WALLET_PAYMENTS_ENABLED=true` in the backend environment.
Run after Stripe confirms the intent succeeded; this finalizes the adoption status in the backend.
